message: Stage -1.2 drafted angle=0 v1 mode=cold-start runner=codex/gpt-5.5 status=completed
Filled Stage 0 theory note for finite-donor distributional synthetic-control identification, confirming row-span algebra and refuting row-span-failure-alone monotone nonidentification.

Review boundary stage_0.5_to_0 routed to stage_neg1 (reject fast-path): Case 6b (unsalvageable conjecture at stage_0.5_to_0) + regression pattern (a) — single review but reviewer explicitly states 'no realistic local repair would lift this kernel to flagship without replacing the theorem'; additionally the locked-parameter block is a panel/synthetic-control object while the run is declared exactid (anchor mismatch), and the main Theorem 1 silently omits Assumption qif so its sharpness claim doesn't apply to the advertised rank-invariant factor causal model. Defects are conjecture-level (wrong cluster anchor, wrong main object), not a derivation step Stage 0 could patch.
Filled the Stage 0 derivation with the sharp donor-weight identified set, row-span identification formula, and monotone escape correction.

Review boundary stage_0.5_to_0 routed to user (reject fast-path): Intervention judge returned no parseable output after 2 attempts (model output did not contain a JSON object); diagnostics dumped to /<repo-root>/CausalSmith/doc/formalization/research/eid_dist_synth_control/eid_dist_synth_control_v1_intervention_raw_1779229636941.txt. Synthesized intervention deterministically from latest review: Latest review at stage_0.5_to_0 is reject with 1 novelty finding(s); tier appears below the orchestrator floor (Assessed tier is below novelty_target=flagship; the kernel is a row-span diagnostic and monotone-envelope correction rather than a flagship theorem, and no local revision would lift it to the required flagship tier.). Synthesized route=user — flagship attainment requires human decision (lower tier, accept conditional, or commission new kernel).

Review boundary stage_0.5_to_0 routed to stage_neg1 (reject fast-path): Case 6b + regression pattern (a): both reviewers reject at the conjecture level — Conjecture 1 is refuted by the note's own three-block finite-support counterexample (row-span failure coexists with Q_DW(P)={0}), Conjecture 2's flagship d_infty-open global monotone-selection claim is explicitly not proved (only a finite-dimensional witness + rank-wise determinant stability), and Theorem 1 collapses to a definition-body restatement of Q_I(P). The kernel is unsalvageable as-conjectured; re-deriving on the same .tex would burn budget. Per-boundary heuristic overridden because the defect is at the proposal layer, not a fixable derivation step.
